name: Maple Walnut Cake
recipeIngredient: 14 ounces Unsalted butter; at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Butter and flour two 9-inch cake pans.
2. In a bowl, combine the flour, baking soda, salt, and baking powder.
3. In another bowl, mix buttermilk and pure maple syrup.
4. Using an electric mixer on medium-low speed, beat 12 tablespoons of butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy, about 5 minutes.
5. Add 1 teaspoon of maple extract and vanilla extract. Beat in eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
6. Reduce the mixer speed and alternate beating in the flour mixture with the buttermilk mixture.
7. Stir in chopped walnuts.
8. Pour batter into prepared pans.
9. Bake for about 25 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Allow cooling for 10 minutes in the pans before removing them and letting cool completely on wire racks.
11. While waiting for the cake to cool, sift confectioner's sugar.
12. Using an electric mixer on medium speed, beat remaining 16 tablespoons of butter until creamy.
13. Reduce speed to low and gradually add confectioner's sugar, beating in the remaining teaspoon of maple extract.
14. Fold in marshmallow creme with a spatula.
15. Place one cake layer on a serving plate and spread frosting over the top.
16. Add the second cake layer and spread frosting over the top and sides.
17. Decorate with walnut halves.
Work Time: 1 hour
Total Time: 1 hour 25 minutes, plus cooling time
